text:
{
  "title": "Contextual Sleep Need Gap",
  "group_name": "contextual_sleep_need_gap",
  "family": "contextual_recovery_residual",
  "summary": "Represent whether a student's sleep amount is unusually deficient, typical, or excessive for their stress, activity, and perceived sleep-quality context, treating recovery mismatch as a distinct risk signal.",
  "depends_on": [],
  "strategy": "Use only feature values, never health_condition. Build training-distribution context tables for sleep_duration among rows with observed sleep, after mapping missing categorical context values to a sentinel. Define activity_demand as the mean of train-percentile ranks of step_count and exercise_duration using available values, bin it into low, middle, and high train-quantile bands, with an unknown band when both are missing. Estimate expected contextual sleep by median sleep_duration for the hierarchy (stress_level, sleep_quality, physical_activity_level, activity_demand_bin, smoking_alcohol), then fallback to (stress_level, sleep_quality, physical_activity_level), then (stress_level, sleep_quality), then global median when a cell has fewer than 50 training rows. For each row create expected_context_sleep, signed sleep gap clipped to [-4, 4], absolute gap, deficit-only gap, surplus-only gap, robust z gap using the matched-cell IQR with global fallback, and a compact state code: missing sleep, context-short by more than 1 hour, context-typical within 1 hour, or context-long by more than 1 hour. Also include CDC adult sleep shortfall max(0, 7 - sleep_duration), based on https://www.cdc.gov/sleep/about/index.html; the context choice is motivated by evidence that exercise, stress, and sleep quality interact in college students, e.g. https://www.dovepress.com/the-impact-of-physical-exercise-on-sleep-quality-among-college-student-peer-reviewed-fulltext-article-PRBM.",
  "expected_signal": "Raw sleep duration can miss whether a value is normal for the surrounding lifestyle pattern; this group may separate resilient students whose sleep is adequate despite high stress from students whose recovery is unexpectedly poor for otherwise favorable habits, improving balanced accuracy on minority fit and unhealthy classes.",
  "risk": "The signal may be redundant with existing sleep, stress, and activity features, and context medians can encode dataset-specific synthetic structure that shifts between train and test; sparse or missing-heavy contexts need fallback handling to avoid unstable residuals."
}
